steps.title: Extract center and radius from circle A
steps.content:
From the standard form $(x - h)^2 + (y - k)^2 = r^2$, the equation $(x - 2)^2 + (y - 3)^2 = 9$ tells us:
  • Center: $(2, 3)$
  • Radius: $r_A = \sqrt{9} = 3$

steps.title: Find circle B's radius and write its equation
steps.content:
Circle B has the same center $(2, 3)$ but radius twice as large: $r_B = 2 \times 3 = 6$.

Squaring the radius: $r_B^2 = 6^2 = 36$.

The equation for circle B is:
$$(x - 2)^2 + (y - 3)^2 = 36$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
